Financial stability of farmers by providing insurance cover to their fruit crops against weather hazards.
Priority under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana in the state is to provide assistance from the point of view of sustainability.
A restructured climate-based Fertility Insurance Scheme is being implemented. Due to various weather hazards,
The productivity of the fruit is adversely affected, leading to a large-scale decline in production.
The farmers face a financial crisis as they do not get the expected yield.
Under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana, as a measure to enable farmers to get fruitful compensation.
Restructured Weather Based Fruit Insurance Scheme, in the state for 2 years 2024-25 and 2025-26,
Mangoes include oranges, melons, cashews, pomegranates, mangoes, bananas, grapes (A and B), strawberries, and on an experimental basis.
The Agricultural Insurance Company of India, Bajaj, has a revenue board in 30 districts for 9 fruits, namely papaya.
General Insurance Limited, Generali Central Insurance Company Limited and Universal Sompo General Insurance Company Limited.
The policy is being implemented by the insurance companies as per the Government Decision in Reference No. (1).
Under the restructured weather-based Fertile Insurance Scheme, the Ambiya Bahar Sun 2024-25 covers hailstorms.
Agriculture as demanded by the Agricultural Insurance Company of India under Reference No. (3) for additional weather hazard.
